name: Circulating Factor Modulation Coverage Repair

scope.question: Do the June 9 circulating-factor coverage gaps require staged public records, and should they change the public boundaries?

summary: Coverage repair staged public records for the June 9 gaps: repeated healthy-adult plasmapheresis limiting biomarker evidence, real-world Alzheimer TPE disease-boundary evidence, young-donor plasma exchange MCI preprint context, microplastics TPE biomarker context, aging and frailty no-results registries, NCT06534450 registry reconciliation, FDA young-plasma safety context, and 2026 plasma/TPE review boundaries. The staged outlook keeps evidence_stage human_functional_benefit, momentum mixed, and confidence low.

materiality_decision.rationale: The repair changes the public source map, trial-status context, intervention taxonomy, safety-boundary support, and outlook support map without changing the stage, momentum, confidence, or rating-change criteria.

search_log.summary: Confirmed registry status, enrollment, completion dates, last-updated dates, and no registry-posted results for the healthy-adult plasmapheresis, aging-biomarker plasmapheresis, frailty TPE, young-donor plasma exchange MCI, and randomized TPE biomarker registries.

Confirmed metadata and interpretation boundaries for the healthy-adult plasmapheresis biomarker study, real-world Alzheimer TPE cohort, plasma rejuvenation translation review, and TPE immune-modulation review.

Confirmed medRxiv young-donor plasma exchange MCI preprint metadata and Journal of Clinical Apheresis microplastics TPE metadata, including the microplastics abstract's high-baseline reduction and low-baseline tubing-leaching limitation.

search_log.query: Important Information about Young Donor Plasma Infusions for Profit
search_log.summary: Confirmed FDA's February 19, 2019 safety communication cautioning that young-donor plasma infusions promoted for aging and disease claims have no proven clinical benefit and carry infectious, allergic, respiratory, cardiovascular, circulatory-overload, and other plasma-product risks.

trial_watch_checks.summary: Completed healthy-adult plasmapheresis registry has no ClinicalTrials.gov results, but a linked peer-reviewed paper is staged as result-bearing limiting evidence.
Unknown-status aging-biomarker plasmapheresis registry has estimated 2022 completion and no posted results.
Unknown-status frailty TPE registry has estimated 2025 completion and no posted results.
Unknown-status young-donor plasma exchange MCI registry has no registry-posted results; a preprint reports pilot safety and feasibility results.
Published randomized TPE biomarker study remains represented by PMID 40424097, while the registry is active not recruiting with no registry-posted results.

excluded_sources.title: Plasma Exchange With Albumin Replacement for Treating Alzheimer's Disease
excluded_sources.reason: Completed AMBAR-lineage historical registry context with posted results, but the public AMBAR finding is already represented by PMID 32715623 and this bounded repair prioritizes missing no-results and limiting-evidence gaps.

excluded_sources.title: Plasma Dilution After Myocardial Ischemia-Reperfusion Injury Promotes Cardiac Repair, Heart Performance, and Recovery of Motor Function and Endurance in Old Mice.
excluded_sources.reason: Recent old-mouse plasma-dilution evidence is relevant preclinical context, but this repair is bounded to human plasma procedure gaps and public taxonomy boundaries.

excluded_sources.title: Periodic Therapeutic Phlebotomy Mitigates Systemic Aging Phenotypes by Promoting Bone Marrow Function.
excluded_sources.reason: Phlebotomy is a neighboring blood-procedure boundary, not one of the public circulating-factor evidence streams repaired in this bundle.

excluded_sources.title: Elevated circulating GDF11 and its role in age-related sarcopenia.
excluded_sources.reason: GDF11 is relevant single-factor boundary context, but the repair already keeps single-factor candidates separated and does not stage a new GDF11 public claim.

notes: The repair stages a public bundle because the missing sources alter source completeness, safety-boundary wording, and intervention taxonomy.
The staged outlook keeps evidence_stage human_functional_benefit, momentum mixed, confidence low, and rating-change criteria unchanged.
PMID 40592961 is staged as limiting evidence for healthy-adult plasmapheresis without replacement, not as a direct contradiction of AMBAR-style albumin-replacement TPE.
next_actions: If reviews pass, publish the repair bundle and rerun planning state.
Resume ordinary surveillance for circulating factor modulation after publication.
